hero.title: Reviving Damascus's Crafts & Living Memory
hero.description: A cultural foundation dedicated to preserving Damascus's artisanal heritage, training a new generation of craftspeople, and safeguarding the city's living memory for generations to come.

about.description: Our story, vision, and mission in preserving Damascene heritage.

training.description: Programs that train artisans and pass traditional skills to new generations.

memory.description: A living archive of Damascus's stories, images, and time-honored crafts.

news.description: Our latest news, events, and announcements about the foundation's work.

contact.description: Get in touch for partnership, support, or inquiries.

hero.title: About Us
hero.summary: Damascene Dream is a nonprofit cultural foundation working to preserve Damascus's artisanal heritage and document the city's memory, through training, archiving, and community partnerships.

links.storyDescription: How the idea began and grew into a foundation working on the ground.
links.teamDescription: The people leading the foundation's mission every day.
links.visionDescription: Where we're headed, and what we aim to achieve.

story.title: Our Story
story.paragraph1: The idea for Damascene Dream began with a group of people passionate about Damascene heritage, who set out to document traditional crafts at risk of disappearing, after noticing a marked decline in practicing artisans and weak interest in passing these skills to new generations.
story.paragraph2: Since our founding, we've built a network of artisans, volunteers, and partner institutions, and launched training and archiving programs aimed at faithfully preserving this living heritage and passing it on, while embracing contemporary tools and methods that keep it accessible to new generations.

team.title: Our Team
team.intro: A group of specialists and volunteers united by a shared passion for Damascene heritage.

vision.title: Our Vision & Goals
vision.intro: We look forward to a future where Damascene crafts remain alive and thriving, and the city's memory is preserved and accessible to all.

goals.goal1: Document endangered Damascene handicrafts before they disappear.
goals.goal2: Train a new generation of artisans and pass on traditional skills.
goals.goal3: Build a living digital archive preserving the city's stories, images, and crafts.
goals.goal4: Strengthen local and international partnerships to sustain this heritage.

hero.title: Training Center
hero.summary: Training programs that pass on Damascene and Armenian handicraft skills to a new generation of artisans, through hands-on workshops and a documented archive of past programs.

links.damasceneDescription: Training programs in traditional Damascene handicrafts.
links.armenianDescription: Training programs in traditional Armenian handicrafts.
links.archiveDescription: A timeline of training programs from 2016 to 2025.
links.currentDescription: Details of the current 2026 training program.

damascene.title: Damascene Crafts
damascene.intro: Training specialties preserving the finest Damascene handicrafts.

s1.title: Damascene Calligraphy
s1.description: Teaching Arabic calligraphy fundamentals in the traditional Damascene style.

s2.title: Mosaic Art
s2.description: Assembling colored mosaic pieces in Damascene decorative patterns.

s3.title: Copper Engraving
s3.description: Engraving copper vessels and plates with traditional hand tools.

s4.title: Mother-of-Pearl Inlay
s4.description: Inlaying wood with mother-of-pearl in the Damascene mosaic technique.

s5.title: Brocade Weaving
s5.description: Weaving fine Damascene fabric with silk and gold threads.

armenian.title: Armenian Crafts
armenian.intro: Training specialties preserving traditional Armenian handicrafts.

s1.title: Silversmithing
s1.description: Shaping jewelry and silver pieces using traditional Armenian techniques.

s2.title: Armenian Embroidery
s2.description: Embroidering fabrics with inherited Armenian stitches and patterns.

s3.title: Carpet Weaving
s3.description: Hand-weaving carpets with authentic Armenian colors and motifs.

s4.title: Armenian Pottery
s4.description: Shaping and decorating ceramic vessels in the classic Armenian style.

archive.title: Program Archive
archive.intro: A timeline of training programs delivered over the years.

timeline.y2016: Founding program to train beginner artisans.
timeline.y2018: Advanced workshops in Damascene engraving and ornamentation.
timeline.y2020: Digital documentation program for veteran artisans' techniques.
timeline.y2022: Training partnership with Armenian craft institutions.
timeline.y2024: Trainer certification program for graduate artisans.
timeline.y2025: Program expansion to include remote workshops.

current.title: 2026 Program
current.description: The 2026 program combines hands-on in-workshop training with field mentorship from experienced artisans, aiming to graduate a new cohort of skilled artisans ready to pass these crafts on in turn.

components.c1: Weekly hands-on workshops with experienced artisans.
components.c2: One-on-one mentoring to track each trainee's progress.
components.c3: Field visits to historic artisan workshops.

hero.title: Memory Bank
hero.summary: A living archive gathering Damascus's photographs, documents, and audio recordings, preserving the city's stories and crafts for generations to come.

hero.summary: Have a question, a suggestion, or want to collaborate with us? Send us a message and we'll get back to you as soon as possible.

hero.title: News
hero.summary: The foundation's latest updates: training programs, Memory Bank additions, partnerships, and community events.

item1.title: The 2026 Training Program Kicks Off
item1.summary: The new cohort of Damascene and Armenian craft trainees began their weekly hands-on workshops, mentored by experienced artisans over the coming months.

item2.title: New Audio Archive Added to the Memory Bank
item2.summary: This month we documented a collection of rare audio recordings of Damascene artisans recounting the stories of their trades, now part of the foundation's living archive.

item3.title: Community Exhibition of Trainees' Work
item3.summary: We hosted an open exhibition showcasing pieces made by program trainees, attended by their families, neighborhood residents, and veteran artisans.

item4.title: New Partnership with Armenian Craft Institutions
item4.summary: We signed a cooperation agreement enabling the exchange of expertise and trainers with partner institutions dedicated to preserving traditional Armenian crafts.

item5.title: A New Batch of Historical Documents Preserved
item5.summary: A collection of documents and correspondence from the late Ottoman and Mandate eras was added to the Memory Bank after restoration and digital cataloging.

item6.title: Call for Volunteers in the Archiving Project
item6.summary: We launched a call to welcome new volunteers to help collect and document photographs and oral histories related to Damascus's artisanal heritage.
